notes: Reproduced on all versions tested (2.88.3, 3.119.2, main). When ReadPixels is called with a null-colorspace destination SKImageInfo (as platform conversion methods do), Skia copies raw linear pixel values without converting to sRGB, causing non-sRGB bitmaps to display with incorrect (darker) colors. This is the core mechanism behind SKBitmapImageSource's incorrect handling of non-sRGB bitmaps. Cross-platform verification skipped because this is a pure API-level color space issue in ReadPixels, not platform-specific.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console repro project with SkiaSharp 2.88.3
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/2466 && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/2466 && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 2.88.3
reproductionSteps.output: Restore succeeded. PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp' version '2.88.3' added.

filesCreated.description: Console project targeting net8.0 with SkiaSharp 2.88.3
Repro code: creates linear color space bitmap, reads pixels with null vs sRGB destination, compares values

reproductionSteps.description: Run repro - creates linear color space bitmap, reads pixels with null vs sRGB destination color space, compares pixel values
reproductionSteps.output:
Source bitmap color space is linear: True

Buggy (null colorspace dest) pixel:   R=147, G=8, B=8, A=255
Correct (sRGB colorspace dest) pixel: R=200, G=50, B=50, A=255

BUG REPRODUCED: Colors differ - null colorspace destination skips conversion
  Expected (sRGB converted): R=200, G=50, B=50
  Got (raw linear values):   R=147, G=8, B=8
  The buggy path copies raw linear values without converting to sRGB,
  causing the displayed image to appear much darker than expected.

versionResults.notes: ReadPixels with null-colorspace destination produces R=147,G=8,B=8 instead of expected R=200,G=50,B=50. Raw linear values copied without conversion.
Same wrong pixel values as 2.88.3. Bug persists in latest stable release.
Same wrong pixel values. Bug persists on main branch.

errorMessages.primaryError: ReadPixels with null-colorspace destination copies raw linear values (R=147,G=8,B=8) instead of sRGB-converted values (R=200,G=50,B=50), causing non-sRGB bitmaps to appear much darker

actionability.reason: Reproduced on all versions including main. The root cause is clear (null color space in destination SKImageInfo used by platform conversion extensions), but a fix needs implementation and testing across all affected platform methods.

workarounds: Convert the bitmap to sRGB color space with PlatformColorType and Premul alpha before assigning to SKBitmapImageSource or calling platform conversion methods

actions.description: Cross-reference related color space issue #2354 (same color space unawareness in SKBitmap.GetPixel)
Confirm reproduction with evidence and provide workaround
